title: Investor sentiment dynamics surrounding the Tesla 5-for-1 stock split in August 2020

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Market anticipation of Q2 earnings.
Estimated: Reaction to August 11 stock split announcement.
Estimated: Retail investor hype leading to split date.

events.description: Tesla officially announced a 5-for-1 stock split in the form of a stock dividend to make shares more accessible to employees and retail investors.
The record date was reached, establishing which shareholders were entitled to receive four additional shares for every one share held.
The distribution date occurred, with the additional shares being issued to shareholders after the close of trading.
Tesla stock began trading on a split-adjusted basis, significantly lowering the per-share price for market participants.

agents.name: Elon Musk
agents.description: CEO of Tesla, primary driver of retail sentiment.

agents.name: Retail Investors
agents.description: High-frequency traders and individual Tesla enthusiasts.

agents.name: Institutional Short Sellers
agents.description: Hedge funds betting against Tesla valuation.

agents.name: Financial Media
agents.description: Mainstream financial news outlets reporting on market volatility.

agents.name: Passive Index Funds
agents.description: Large funds forced to rebalance based on index inclusion.

agents.name: Tech Enthusiasts
agents.description: Social media users promoting Tesla's long-term vision.

notes: Pro-side is defined as investors holding a bullish sentiment toward Tesla stock (expecting growth/value). Against-side is defined as investors holding a bearish sentiment (expecting decline/overvaluation). Data is estimated based on market trends and news sentiment analysis during the Q3 2020 period.
